title: premna bracteata
intro: Premna bracteata, also known as the Indian lilac, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Premna bracteata include its ability to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Premna bracteata can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies in the form of a poultice or topical application.
